name: PocketBase auth context behavior
pocketBaseNote: PocketBase-style record rules treat missing, expired, or revoked auth tokens as an empty @request.auth context, while explicit auth endpoints still require a valid token.

cases.name: anonymous request has empty auth context
cases.pocketBaseNote: Without an auth token, @request.auth fields resolve as empty values and ownership rules do not match.

cases.name: valid auth token populates request auth context
cases.pocketBaseNote: A valid auth token populates @request.auth so owner-based list rules can match the requesting record.

cases.name: expired auth token becomes empty rule auth context
cases.pocketBaseNote: Expired bearer tokens should not grant auth context to record rules; they behave like an anonymous request.

cases.name: expired auth token cannot refresh
cases.pocketBaseNote: Auth endpoints that explicitly operate on a token still reject expired tokens.

cases.name: revoked auth token becomes empty rule auth context
cases.pocketBaseNote: A revoked bearer token should not populate @request.auth for record rule evaluation.

cases.name: revoked auth token cannot refresh
cases.pocketBaseNote: Revoked tokens remain invalid for explicit auth refresh operations.
